title: Loops
description: Repeat code with for and while loops

blocks.content: Loops let you repeat code multiple times without writing it over and over. Use them to process lists of items, count, or repeat an action.
A `for` loop repeats code a specific number of times. It tracks a counter variable that changes each iteration.

blocks.title: Breaking Down the For Loop
blocks.content:
- **Initialize** `let i = 0`: Create and start a counter at 0
- **Condition** `i < 5`: Keep looping while this is true
- **Increment** `i++`: Add 1 to i after each iteration
A `while` loop repeats code as long as a condition is true. Use it when you don't know how many times to loop in advance.

blocks.title: Infinite Loops
blocks.content: Be careful! If your loop condition is always true and never changes, the loop will run forever and freeze your program.

- `break` stops the loop immediately
- `continue` skips to the next iteration
An easy way to loop through items in an array without managing a counter.
Choose the right loop: Use `for` when you know the number of iterations. Use `while` when the condition is more complex. Use `for...of` for arrays.
